events.title: Lizzie and the Makers & Paisley Fields Album Release Party
events.description:
Tickets: https://wl.eventim.us/Getitrightthefirsttime
Each ticket purchase includes 12-ounce beer, 7 ounce wine, well cocktail or Non-alcoholic beverage. Additional beverages can be purchased for reasonable prices!
EVENT INFO
Album Release Garden Party (but make it weird): “Are U Mad At Me” x “Everything’s Fine”
Paisley Fields and Lizzie and the Makers are throwing it down to celebrate the May release of their new albums. Live sets, red carpet, free flash tats and a drink with your ticket, DIY hat chaos, local art sales, and all the spring sun you can handle (rain or shine).
Outdoor. Unhinged. Fun guaranteed.
Come weird. Come loud. Come ready.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ Cash bar with themed cocktails.
Champagne Poneys kick things off at 2pm!
Event Info
Date: May 3, 2026
Doors: 1:30pm
Showtime:2:00pm
End time (sorry): 6:00pm
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N
CHAMPAGNE PONEYS
The Champagne Poneys are a captivating vocal trio featuring Holland Belle (Catskill, NY), Allison Godleski (Ghent, NY), and Helen Rose (Potter Hollow, NY). Their sound evokes the classic country trios of the 20th century, with rich, nuanced three-part harmonies and dynamic vocal ranges that highlight the distinct texture of each singer’s voice. Each member of the Poneys is a songwriter in her own right, bringing a delightful diversity to their original songs. They draw deep from the wells of country & western, folk, and honky-tonk traditions, with lyrics exploring themes of nature, love and classic storytelling.
PAISLEY FIELDS
Paisley Fields wants to know — in stretched out audio, in hyperpop gibberish, in plain voice, and in song — Are U Mad At Me?
Are U Mad At Me, due out May 1st via Don Giovanni Records, marks a huge, cowboy boot-wearing step for the Brooklyn-based songwriter. Fields proves that genre is as fluid as sexuality, as the album saunters across country, pop, disco, grunge, and classical with ease.
LIZZIE AND THE MAKERS
On their 2026 Everything’s Fine EP, Lizzie & The Makers unveil a nuanced reinvention of their already singular blend indie rock, pop, and airy introspection. With golden-voiced front woman Lizzie Edwards the New York quintet’s primary songwriter, Everything’s Fine’s five songs are more overtly hooky and keyboards-forward than prior Makers material, draping epic, emotive melodies in otherworldly, subtly unsettling auras.
